seo.title: AI Agent Drift Monitoring in OpenAI Agents SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent drift monitoring in OpenAI Agents SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Drift in OpenAI Agents SDK manifests as agents deviating from intended behaviors during multi-agent handoffs, creating infinite loops, bypassing guardrails through prompt injection, or consuming unvalidated tool outputs. These issues are particularly prevalent in production systems where specialized agents hand off tasks without proper state validation or tracing. The SDK's flexible handoff patterns and async execution make drift detection challenging without systematic monitoring.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
howItManifests.symptom: Agents ping-pong tasks indefinitely without task completion
howItManifests.impact: Infinite API calls, escalating costs, and system hangs

howItManifests.symptom: Injected prompts override agent instructions during handoffs

howItManifests.scenario: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
howItManifests.symptom: Agents process malformed or malicious tool responses

howItManifests.scenario: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
howItManifests.symptom: Lost visibility into which agent caused drift
howItManifests.impact: Impossible to debug or attribute failures

detection.description: Monitors agent handoff patterns to detect circular dependencies

fixes.name: Handoff Circuit Breaker
fixes.description: Prevents infinite loops with max handoff limits
fixes.code:
from openai_agents import Agent, Runner
from contextlib import contextmanager

@contextmanager
def handoff_circuit_breaker(max_handoffs=10):
    handoff_count = 0
    try:
        yield handoff_count
    finally:
        handoff_count += 1
        if handoff_count > max_handoffs:
            raise Exception('Handoff limit exceeded - circuit breaker triggered')

# Agent configuration with breaker
research_agent = Agent(
    name='researcher',
    instructions='...',
    handoff_limit=3,  # SDK built-in limit
    max_iterations=10
)

runner = Runner(agents=[research_agent], circuit_breaker=True)

fixes.name: Input Sanitizer Guardrail
fixes.description: Strips dangerous prompts before agent processing

fixes.name: Tracing Interceptor
fixes.description: Captures full handoff chains for debugging
fixes.code:
from openai_agents import Runner, TraceEvent
from opentelemetry import trace

class DriftTracer:
    def __init__(self):
        self.tracer = trace.get_tracer('agent-drift')
    
    async def trace_handoff(self, event: TraceEvent):
        if event.type == 'AGENT_HANDOFF':
            span = self.tracer.start_span('agent_handoff')
            span.set_attribute('from_agent', event.from_agent)
            span.set_attribute('to_agent', event.to_agent)
            span.set_attribute('input_tokens', len(event.input))
            span.set_attribute('handoff_depth', len(event.trace_stack))
            
            if len(event.trace_stack) > 10:
                span.record_exception(Exception('Deep handoff chain'))
            
            span.end()

tracer = DriftTracer()
runner = Runner(agents=[...], trace_handler=tracer.trace_handoff)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Drift Monitoring Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade drift monitoring by integrating as an output guardrail across all OpenAI Agents SDK workflows. It automatically detects handoff loops by tracking agent interaction graphs, identifies prompt injections through semantic analysis of handoff messages, and validates tool outputs against expected schemas. Vex's real-time alerting and automated remediation rules prevent drift from reaching production impact.

The integration requires adding VexGuardrail to each agent, which captures 100% of handoff events, tool calls, and state transitions. Vex maintains a baseline of normal agent behavior learned from your production traffic, flagging deviations like unexpected handoffs or schema violations. Remediation can be fully automated - Vex can terminate looping agents, sanitize injected prompts, or reroute tasks to fallback agents.

Vex dashboards provide drill-down visibility into drift incidents, showing the exact handoff chain, injected prompt, or malformed output that caused the issue. This eliminates manual tracing and debugging across complex multi-agent systems.
vexAutomation.code:
pip install openai-agents vex-sdk

from openai_agents import Agent, Runner, Guardrail
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

# Initialize Vex guardrail
vex_guard = VexGuardrail(
    api_key='vex-your-key',
    detectors=[
        'handoff_loops',
        'prompt_injection',
        'tool_schema_drift',
        'state_drift'
    ],
    auto_remediate=True
)

# Apply to research agent
researcher = Agent(
    name='researcher',
    model='gpt-4o',
    instructions='Research topics...',
    guardrails=[vex_guard]
)

# Apply to all agents in workflow
coordinator = Agent(
    name='coordinator',
    instructions='Manage handoffs...',
    guardrails=[vex_guard]
)

# Production runner
runner = Runner(
    agents=[researcher, coordinator],
    max_parallel=5
)

result = runner.run('Analyze market trends')

# Vex auto-generates alerts and fixes drift
